description: Template for subagent briefs. The primary agent fills the slot fields when spawning each subagent.
preamble: You are exploring a single solution path for a coding problem. Your job is to reason deeply about ONE specific approach and report back in structured form.

slots.label: The Problem
slots.description: Full problem statement, including constraints and any examples. If the problem has stated constraints (e.g., 'memory-limited', 'must handle streaming'), include them verbatim for every subagent.

slots.label: Your Assigned Approach
slots.description: The specific approach this subagent should explore.

subfields.label: Approach name
subfields.description: E.g., 'Segment tree with lazy propagation'. Vary wording across subagents to avoid accidentally constraining two toward the same direction.

subfields.label: Approach description
subfields.description: 1-2 sentences describing what this approach means at a high level, enough that the subagent understands the direction without being over-constrained.

slots.label: What NOT to do
slots.description: Optional hint if worried a subagent will drift toward another path's territory (e.g., 'Do not use a heap in this path').

slots.label: Maximum steps
slots.description: Maximum number of reasoning steps the subagent may take. Constrains subagent execution budget.

slots.label: Maximum output lines
slots.description: Maximum number of lines in the subagent's report output. Constrains response size.

taskInstructions.description: Instructions given to the subagent describing what to produce.

sections.label: Core idea
sections.instruction: What is the key insight that makes this approach work?

sections.label: Step-by-step walkthrough
sections.instruction: Trace through the algorithm on a concrete small example.

sections.label: Complexity analysis
sections.instruction: Time and space, with justification (not just labels).

sections.label: Edge cases
sections.instruction: Enumerate at least 3; explain how this approach handles each.

sections.label: Implementation considerations
sections.instruction: Non-obvious choices a developer would face.

sections.label: Honest strengths and weaknesses
sections.instruction: Where does this shine, where does it struggle?

taskInstructions.outputConstraint: Return ONLY the structured report. Do not add preamble or conclusion outside it.

primaryAgentNotes: Vary the 'assigned approach' wording across subagents — don't accidentally constrain two of them toward the same direction with similar framing.

If the problem has stated constraints (e.g., 'memory-limited', 'must handle streaming'), include them verbatim in the problem statement for every subagent — they must all reason against the same constraints.
You may optionally add a 'What NOT to do' hint if you're worried a subagent will drift toward another path's territory (e.g., 'Do not use a heap in this path').
